title: Maveropepimut-S, pembrolizumab and low dose cyclophosphamide in metastatic ovarian cancer: phase 1/2 PESCO trial
authors: Ana C. Veneziani
Stephanie Lheureux
Douglas G. Millar
Neesha Dhani

Judy Quintos
Lisa Wang
Xuan Li
Valerie Bowering
Pam Ohashi
Ben Wang
Amit M. Oza
abstract: Published online: 14 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72125-0 Epithelial ovarian cancer (EOC) frequently relapses with rising chemoresistance. This phase 1/2 trial evaluates MVP-S vaccination plus pembrolizumab and cyclophosphamide in recurrent EOC, assessing safety, clinical efficacy outcomes, and MVP-S-specific immunity.

title: Electrolyte Parameter Identification by High-Frequency Impedance Measurement During Current Pulses
authors: Conrad Bieneck, Lars von Kolzenberg, Carsten G. Staacke, Felix Riewald, Arnulf Latz and Timo Danner
abstract: Accurate electrolyte transport parameters are essential for reliable simulations of lithium-ion battery performance. This study presents a novel method to determine the diffusion coefficient and cationic transference number of liquid electrolytes by measuring high-frequency impedance spectra during pulse polarization. The pulse induces concentration polarization within the separator, which is detectable in changes in the high-frequency resistance. A pseudo-2D electrochemical model is fitted to the temporal evolution of the high-frequency resistance to extract the diffusion coefficient and tran…

title: A Bilayer Cathode Design Procedure for Li Ion Batteries Using the Multilayer Doyle-Fuller-Newman Model (M-DFN)
authors: E. C. Tredenick, A. M. Boyce, R. Drummond and S. R. Duncan
abstract: Heterogeneities in lithium ion batteries can be significant factors in electrode under utilisation and degradation while charging. Bilayer electrodes have been proposed as a convenient and scalable way to homogenise the electrode response. In this paper, the design of a bilayer cathode for Li-ion batteries composed of separate layers of lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide Li[Ni0.6Mn0.2Co0.2]O2 (NMC622) and lithium iron phosphate LiFePO4 (LFP) is optimised using the multilayer Doyle-Fuller-Newman (M-DFN) model. Changes to the carbon binder domain, electrolyte volume fraction, and tortuosity p…

title: Interfacial Mechanics Regulation in Electrochemical Impedance Analysis of Anode-Free Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Zhiqiang Ling, Qian Zhang, Chuanwei Li, Siyuan Yang, Lipan Xin, Zhiyong Wang, Lin’an Li and Shibin Wang
abstract: Anode-free solid-state lithium metal batteries (AFSSLBs) form lithium metal anodes through in situ electrodeposition on the current collector surface during cycling. This design not only achieves higher energy density but also avoids the complex manufacturing processes associated with traditional lithium metal anodes. However, inhomogeneous lithium deposition during operation can lead to interfacial mechanical failure, severely degrading the battery’s electrochemical stability. This study investigates the correlation between interfacial mechanical properties and electrochemical performance in …

title: Galvanic Corrosion Behavior Between Copper (T2) and Carbon Steel (Q235) in Simulated Deep Geological Environment
authors: Ziang Su, Jie Pang, Yu Xin, Nazhen Liu, Xiangju Liu, Xiaoxuan Han, Weichen Xu and Xuwei Chen
abstract: In this study, the galvanic corrosion behavior of Cu and CS (carbon steel) in simulated Beishan groundwater was investigated. The influence of Cu:CS area ratio (up to 8000), temperature (20–70 °C), O2 content (groundwater sparged with air, N2, and in a N2-filled glove box), on galvanic current density (ig) was investigated. In the presence of sufficient O2, the slope of steady-state galvanic current density (ig∞) to Cu:CS area ratio indicates the segmented control process of galvanic corrosion, which are O2 reduction kinetic control, O2 diffusion control, and anodic reaction control. As Cu:CS …

title: Investigating a Localized High Concentration Electrolyte for Silicon-Containing Lithium-Ion Cells
authors: Jeffin James Abraham, Eytan Mendel-Elias, Saar Omessi, Chanmonirath (Michael) Chak, Lin Ma, Jessie Harlow, Michel B. Johnson, Michael Metzger and J. R. Dahn
abstract: Energy-dense lithium-ion (Li-ion) batteries are increasingly in demand for a wide range of energy storage applications. The introduction of silicon (Si) as a negative electrode candidate offers a promising route to meeting the high energy density requirements of next-generation Li-ion batteries. However, suitable electrolytes must be identified that can ensure both long cycle life and cell safety. In this study, a localized high-concentration electrolyte (LHCE) was evaluated for three different Si-containing negative electrode types: Chemical SiC (ChemSiC), micron-sized Si (μSi), and Mechanica…
